title: DOE Announces $6.57 Billion Loan to Rivian for Georgia EV Plant
text: The Department of Energy's Loan Programs Office announced the closing of a $6.57 billion loan ($5.975 billion of principal and $592 million of capitalized interest) to Rivian New Horizon, LLC to finance the development and construction of Project Horizon, an electric vehicle manufacturing facility located in Stanton Springs North, near the City of Social Circle, Georgia. The loan will support construction of a nine million square foot facility to manufacture up to 400,000 mass-market electric sport utility vehicles and SUVs and crossover vehicles annually, boosting the regional economy and helping build America's clean transportation future. Project Horizon will manufacture mid-sized electric SUVs in Georgia that are price competitive with gas-powered equivalents. At its Georgia facility, Rivian will produce its all-electric midsize platform with its R2 and R3 models as the first variants in production. Rivian has renegotiated a loan from the U.S. Department of Energy from a prior amount of $6.57 billion down to $4.5 billion. The changes enable Rivian to draw on the loan sooner and have greater initial production but lower total production capacity for the plant amid uncertain demand for all-electric vehicles.
